title: carex dioica subsp. custoriana
intro: Carex dioica subsp. custoriana, also known as the Wood Sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex dioica sedge plant that is native to Europe and North America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other conditions. The health benefits of Carex dioica subsp. custoriana include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, flu, and respiratory issues. Carex dioica subsp. custoriana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
